mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production directly expands real output and corporate profits, providing a persistent tailwind that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases month-over-month yet the median 20-day SPX return is negative or zero, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market weakness, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment that drives negative short-term equity returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: If UNRATE jumps >0.2pp but the 5-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate and CPI are jointly elevated, the combined effect of high nominal rates and high inflation tightens real financial conditions beyond the sum of individual effects, compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL) does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient in predictive regressions for 60-day forward returns (p>0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In early economic expansions, month-over-month retail sales growth exceeding 2% signals a surge in consumer demand that drives earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If during early expansions (defined as first 24 months after recession trough) the trigger produces median 20-day SPX return <0, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong annualized GDP growth above 3% indicates robust aggregate demand that boosts corporate profitability and leads to positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If GDP growth >3% annualized is followed by median 60-day SPX returns below 1%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains exceeding 200,000 signal a tight labor market that raises consumer income and spending, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases >200k and the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate but industrial production decelerates, the demand-supply imbalance builds inventory pressure and signals potential earnings disappointments, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth >1% and INDPRO month-over-month growth <0%
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs and the median 20-day SPX return is positive, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, financial conditions become excessively restrictive in late-cycle tightening phases, historically triggering short-term equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS >5% and the 5-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of instances during late-cycle regimes, reject.
